steps.title: Focus on audience size
steps.content: The underlined claim is about increasing the number of people who experience a work after it is placed online. Evidence for that claim needs to address audience size, not artistic quality, the artist's other works, or general opinions about the museum. The strongest support would compare online viewing with in-person access.

steps.title: Reject unrelated evidence
steps.content:
• Choice A - The Minneapolis Institute of Art has several works by Neda Farhadi.: this is about the museum's holdings, not how many people experience any one work.
  • Choice B - Many people who have been to Minneapolis say that the Minneapolis Institute of Art is worth visiting.: this concerns the museum's reputation, not online viewers of a specific artwork.
  • Choice C - Mississippi Delta has been praised by some art critics.: praise from critics does not show that more people experience the work.
  • Choice D - Each year, more people access the online image of Mississippi Delta than visit the Minneapolis Institute of Art in person.: this directly shows that online availability can reach more people than in-person museum access.
The answer is D.
